title: Numbers and Counting
description: Learn Bulgarian numbers, common counting patterns, and how to use them in real situations.

content.title: The First Numbers
content.description: Start with the basic numbers you will use every day.

content.title: Counting Beyond Ten
content.description: Learn the teens and a few important multiples.

content.title: A useful pattern
bullets: Teen numbers often build from the base number plus a teen ending.
Tens are important for prices, ages, and time.
Practice the whole number as a unit, not only the translation.

content.title: Numbers in Real Life
content.description: Use numbers in common everyday phrases.

content.note: Here 'две' is used because the noun is feminine.
A short way to tell the time.
Very practical for shops and markets.

content.title: Practice
content.description: A quick reminder and a self-check.

content.title: Study tip
content.text: Practice counting aloud from one to twenty, then jump to the tens and prices you hear in shops.

content.question: What is the Bulgarian word for 'twelve'?
content.note: It is a good number to memorize early because it appears in dates and prices.
